summary_of_threads_started_this_week:
Junhyuk Lee's research paper examines the economic conditions under which Bitcoin miners might deviate from honest behavior as block rewards diminish, identifying a critical threshold that could incentivize such deviations. The paper proposes several mitigation strategies to stabilize miner incentives and maintain network integrity, such as base fees and adaptive block sizing, which are critical in a fee-only regime. These findings and proposed solutions are discussed in detail, with further insights available at [arxiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05503) and discussions on [Research on Bitcoin Post-Block Rewards](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/research-bitcoin-after-block-rewards-preventing-miners-deviation-when-the-bitcoin-rewards-is-zero/2626).

The release of Bitcoin Core version 31.1 introduces significant improvements, including bug fixes, performance enhancements, and better privacy features. Users upgrading to this version should follow specific installation procedures for different operating systems to ensure a smooth transition. The release, detailed at [Bitcoin Core's official website](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/), highlights the community's ongoing efforts to enhance the software's functionality and security.

A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP) by Jonas Nick, Tim Ruffing, and Yannick Seurin introduces a method for full-aggregation of BIP 340 signatures, aiming to increase transaction efficiency by reducing sizes. This proposal, particularly beneficial for Cross-Input Signature Aggregation (CISA), is open for community feedback and further discussion at [Fabian's BIPs repo fork](https://github.com/fjahr/bips/pull/4). The development reflects a continuous effort to advance cryptographic practices within Bitcoin’s framework, fostering potential improvements in security and operational efficiency.

new_threads_this_week.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards: preventing miner deviation as the block rewards approaches zero
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- The paper analyzes security risks for Bitcoin when shifting to a fee-only model.
- It finds a specific economic threshold that may cause miners to behave dishonestly.
- Proposed strategies aim to stabilize incentives and maintain network integrity.

new_threads_this_week.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin Core 31.1 features bug fixes, performance boosts, and new translations.
- Users must shut down the old version completely before upgrading to the new one.
- The update supports Linux Kernel 3.17, macOS 14, and Windows 10 version 1903 or newer.

new_threads_this_week.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
new_threads_this_week.summary: - A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal introduces efficient signature aggregation methods.  - The proposal, developed by Jonas Nick and others, focuses on Cross-Input Signature Aggregation.  - Stakeholders are invited to review and discuss the proposal via GitHub repositories.

new_threads_this_week.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Game-theory explores strategic implications of quantum computing on Bitcoin's security and economics.
- Potential collusion between quantum entities and miners could threaten blockchain with deep reorganizations.
- The necessity for enhanced research to develop quantum-resistant blockchain mechanisms is highlighted.

new_threads_this_week.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 31.1 introduces performance and security enhancements.
- Upgrade requires complete shutdown of the old version before installing the new one.
- The software is tested across major operating systems, ensuring broad compatibility.

new_threads_this_week.title: Fee Selection for CTV-Based Mining Pool Fanouts
new_threads_this_week.summary: - The qbit pool integrates post-quantum signatures and utilizes TIDES-style accounting.  - Payouts primarily occur through CTV fanouts, keeping coinbase output sizes manageable.  - Transaction fee decisions are challenging, considering volatility and operational specifics.

new_threads_this_week.title: Segwit commitment to post-quantum witness data?
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- Enhancing blockchain with post-quantum cryptography proposes increasing block size for signature data.
- Adding new transaction identifiers and block commitments necessitates extensive network and mining updates.
- Alternative methods include integrating post-quantum data within existing structures to mitigate impact.

new_threads_this_week.title: Babilonia - probabilistic coinjoin and covert betting
new_threads_this_week.summary:
- A new protocol increases privacy and efficiency in cryptocurrency transactions.
- It uses simple Zero-Knowledge Proof techniques for operation and features indistinct pot sweeteners.
- Protocol details are available for review, enhancing swaps and addressing Sybil attacks.

active_posts_this_week.title: DahLIAS: Discrete Logarithm-Based Interactive Aggregate Signatures
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The discussions emphasize the foundational role of AOMDL under ROM in Bitcoin's security claims.
- Nonce generation in BIP327 involves complexities and potential security risks with deterministic nonces.
- Flexibility in statelessness for signers could improve implementation without harming security.

active_posts_this_week.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts_this_week.summary: - The majority of Bitcoin node operators retain the entire blockchain to support new nodes.  - Some operators might prefer selectively pruning non-essential data while maintaining blockchain consensus.  - The discussed proposal for changing Bitcoin data handling lacks significant community support.

active_posts_this_week.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- P2TRH integrates into P2MR, enhancing flexibility in cryptographic applications.
- The integration supports various signatures and strong cryptographic frameworks.
- This strategic merger could lead to advancements in cryptography's efficiency and security.

active_posts_this_week.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts_this_week.summary: - Fabian submitted a comprehensive draft for Bitcoin improvements on the developers' group.  - His research on Crypto Improvement Security Audit is detailed in a report by the Human Rights Foundation.  - These contributions are crucial for enhancing Bitcoin security and operational functionality.

active_posts_this_week.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The proposed dynamic block size could impact blockchain efficiency based on transaction types and fee levels.
- High fees could reduce block size and network throughput, potentially decreasing overall network efficiency.
- The adoption of post-quantum signatures and scaling technologies depends on consistent transaction demand.

active_posts_this_week.title: Scaling Noncustodial Mining Payouts with CTV
active_posts_this_week.summary: - A recent blockchain update incorporates CheckTemplateVerify, sparking discussions on fee selection.  - A dedicated forum thread explores optimizing fee strategies for CTV-based mining pool fanouts.  - This conversation is crucial for developers and stakeholders in cryptocurrency mining.

active_posts_this_week.title: Input-triggered transaction expiry
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The email explores implementing expiry mechanisms in blockchain, especially Bitcoin.
- It suggests `nLockTime` for intrinsic expiry integration, enhancing transaction simplicity.
- The method preserves validation frameworks and debates on consensus-enforced delay lengths.

active_posts_this_week.title: Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
active_posts_this_week.summary: - Low-density parity check codes introduce unnecessary computational overheads.  - Peer limits restrict scalability and function in data systems.  - Historical inefficiencies remain unresolved, hampering system performance.

active_posts_this_week.title: zkPoH: Zero-Knowledge Proof-of-Hodl
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- Recent zk technology developments in Bitcoin have been impressively notable.
- Growing attention on zk innovations highlights their impact on Bitcoin's capabilities.
- This progress points to enhanced privacy and efficiency in blockchain technology.

active_posts_this_week.title: Expiring HTLCs without free relay
active_posts_this_week.summary:
- The email proposes introducing relay-safe expiry to counter time-sensitive attacks in blockchain transactions.
- It discusses reconsidering the 100 block delay in `OP_EXPIRE` based on @ajtowns' suggestions.
- Plans for further research on blockchain transaction improvements were announced for a future blog post.
